assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: Response includes specific commands to check for deprecated API calls with kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep deprecated and mentions checking GKE deprecation insights

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: Response dedicates a specific section to admission webhook compatibility with commands to check webhook configurations and events, plus immediate mitigation steps

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Response includes kubectl top pods -n kube-system to check system component resource usage and mentions that components like coredns may have different resource requirements in 1.32

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Response includes specific commands: kubectl get pods -n kube-system and explicitly mentions looking for 'crashlooping or restarting system components'

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: Response includes kubectl get --raw /metrics commands for both deprecated API calls and API server request duration metrics

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: Response includes a dedicated section 'Service Mesh / Network-Specific Checks' with commands to check Istio/ASM control plane compatibility and proxy injection issues

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: Response includes multiple commands to check events: kubectl get events in kube-system and across all namespaces, though it doesn't explicitly mention scheduling delays or readiness probe failures

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: Response includes a dedicated section on HPA/VPA behavioral changes with commands to check HPA status and scaling decisions, mentioning that K8s 1.32 may have changed HPA algorithm defaults
